Figure 1:Relative LM-head GPU timeTheadT_{\mathrm{head}}for batch size11across models; lower is better.
The underlyingTheadT_{\mathrm{head}}values are normalized with respect to the full vocabulary baseline, set to1.01.0.
VocabTrim reduces the draft vocabulary to6464K tokens.
For SpecVocab and SlimSpec the low rank is set tor=d/8r=d/8, whereddis target model hidden size.
Both VocabTrim and SpecVocab can reduce LM-head latency by only about60%60\%, while SlimSpec achieves an approximately4-5×4\text{-}5\timesreduction.

Figure 2:Drafter latency decomposition at batch sizes11and6464for EAGLE-3 with full-vocabulary projection. Each horizontal bar breaks downTdraftT_{\mathrm{draft}}into main block computation timeTbackboneT_{\mathrm{backbone}}and LM-head computation timeTheadT_{\mathrm{head}}for each target model, measured in microseconds.

Figure 3:End-to-end speedup decomposition in the(ν,ρτ)(\nu,\rho_{\tau})plane for Llama-3.1-8B with temperature0at batch size11(κ=0.25\kappa=0.25).
Dashed lines are theoretical speedup level curves derived from equation4.
The shaded region indicates no end-to-end improvement over the full-vocabulary baseline.
SlimSpec (red stars) achieves the largest LM-head acceleration while keepingρτ\rho_{\tau}close to11.
